goal: Add a safe readiness endpoint at GET /health/maya-gate so the Free Render bluepilot-builder service can verify Bluepilot -> maya-core gate binding without shell access. The endpoint calls the existing Maya gate client functions with harmless probe payloads, never serializes secrets, and never starts a Builder task, write, push, or deploy.
scope_out: Do not modify the Maya gate client or gate logic.
Do not add a route that starts a Builder task, write_file, push, deploy, provider call, or orchestrator path.
Do not log, return, or echo MAYA_CORE_GATE_TOKEN or MAYA_BUILDER_GATE_TOKEN values.
Do not add dependencies or package changes.
Do not modify soulmatch or maya-core.
claims: GET /health/maya-gate is handled in builder/src/health.ts using the existing health handler pattern.
The probe calls assessBudget, assessCorridor, and recordCost from builder/src/mayaBuilderGateClient.ts; it does not construct its own fetch/auth path.
The corridor probe uses actionKind 'push' with dryRun true, not the invalid actionKind 'dry_run'.
The response includes mayaCoreConfigured and per-gate reachable/reason/status fields, but never includes secret values.
The probe performs no irreversible Builder action. recordCost is a tiny best-effort maya-core readiness write and is documented as the only side effect.
assumptions: The Free Render plan blocks shell access, so a browser-callable readiness endpoint is the lowest-risk verification path.
maya-core builder routes are deployed and return 401 without a token, so the remaining question is token binding.
The model openai/gpt-4.1-nano exists in maya-core provider specs and is cheap enough for a synthetic readiness payload.
dod: GET /health/maya-gate returns JSON with mayaCoreConfigured plus budget, corridor, and cost probe results.
Unit tests cover all gates reachable and gate unavailable cases, and assert a fake token value is not serialized.
The probe uses actionKind 'push' with dryRun true and does not introduce a new 'dry_run' literal.
No Builder execution/orchestrator/write-path module is imported by builder/src/health.ts.
npm run typecheck in builder/ passes.
stop_conditions: The implementation would require modifying the gate client, providers, orchestrator, or write path.
A secret value would need to be logged, returned, or committed.
A dependency or package-lock change becomes necessary.
The probe cannot be made harmless without starting a real Builder action.
prior_task_findings: BP-132 added /health and /health/db. BP-133 anchored the live Render deploy. BP-134 scrubbed old BP-131 identifiers from the current working tree. Render Free blocks bluepilot-builder shell access, so BP-135 adds an in-process readiness probe instead of requiring shell access.
